purpose: Returns control to the previous program by setting up the commarea and issuing an XCTL.
Processes the ENTER key by validating input, reading the authorization record, and preparing for screen population.
Sends the authorization details screen to the user, optionally erasing the screen first based on the flag.
Receives the data from the authorization view screen entered by the user.
Processes the ENTER key by validating input, reading the authorization record, and preparing for screen population.
Sends the authorization view screen to the user, optionally erasing the screen first based on the SEND-ERASE-FLG flag.
Resets the commarea to return control to the previous program by XCTLing to the program specified in CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM.
Marks the selected authorization as fraudulent or removes fraud status, updates the fraud record, and refreshes the authorization details.
Sends the authorization view screen to the user, optionally erasing the screen first based on the SEND-ERASE-FLG flag.
Processes the PF8 key to navigate to the next authorization record for the account, updating screen data or displaying a message if at the end.
Sends the authorization view screen to the user, optionally erasing the screen based on the SEND-ERASE-YES flag.
Processes the ENTER key by validating input account and authorization key, reading the corresponding record, and preparing details for display.
Sends the authorization view screen to the user, optionally erasing the screen based on the SEND-ERASE-YES flag.
Prepares to read an authorization record by moving the account ID and authorization key into IMS PCB fields for database access.
Commits the current unit of work in CICS by issuing a SYNCPOINT to ensure data consistency.
Populates the authorization details screen fields using data from the PA segment and working storage after validating no error flag is set.
Sets the account ID and authorization key in the IMS PCB for reading the corresponding authorization record from the database.
Updates the authorization details in the IMS database with fraud status changes and performs a syncpoint or rollback based on the outcome.
Performs a CICS rollback to undo database changes when an error occurs during fraud update processing.
Performs a CICS rollback to undo database changes when an error occurs during fraud update processing.
Populates the authorization details screen with data from the current authorization record and formats related fields for display.
Sets up the account ID and authorization key in the IMS PCB for reading a specific authorization record.
Reads the next authorization record from the IMS database and updates EOF or error status based on the result.
Commits the current unit of work in CICS to ensure data consistency.
Populates the authorization details screen with data from the current authorization record and formats related fields for display.
Populates the screen header with current date, time, transaction name, program name, and title information.
Schedules the IMS PSB and handles errors by setting status flags and displaying an error message if scheduling fails.
Sends the authorization view screen with error message and current header information, optionally erasing the screen first.
Sends the authorization view screen with error message and current header information, optionally erasing the screen first.
Displays the authorization view screen with an error message when a system error occurs while reading the next authorization record.
Commits the current transaction unit of work to ensure data integrity after successfully updating an authorization detail in IMS.
Rolls back the current transaction unit of work due to an error encountered while updating an authorization detail in IMS.
Sends a message to the terminal screen to inform the user of an error condition during fraud tagging, using the content in WS-MESSAGE and controlling screen erase behavior based on SEND-ERASE-YES.
Displays an error message on the terminal screen when PSB scheduling fails, using the message stored in WS-MESSAGE and respecting the SEND-ERASE-YES flag for screen handling.
